title: eSIM vs International Roaming - What Really Costs Less?
description: Are you planning an international trip and facing a dilemma about whether to use your carrier's roaming or buy an eSIM?
body:
Reading time: 5 minutes

The Complete Guide to Making the Smart Decision

Are you planning an international trip and facing a dilemma about whether to use your carrier's roaming or buy an eSIM? Let's break down the numbers and see what really pays off in different scenarios.

Understanding International Roaming Costs

Most Israeli cellular providers now offer roaming packages that seem convenient at first glance. Daily packages typically cost between thirty to seventy shekels per day, which can add up quickly over a week-long trip. Weekly packages range between one hundred fifty to three hundred shekels per week, which sounds better but still accumulates for longer trips. The Pay As You Go rates can reach up to fifty shekels per megabyte, which is outrageously expensive if you accidentally use data without a package. The main disadvantage of roaming is that you pay even for days when you don't use much data, making it inefficient for travelers with variable usage patterns.

The eSIM Revolution

eSIM technology has revolutionized communication for travelers by offering several key advantages. You can purchase and install the eSIM from home before your trip, eliminating airport stress. The technology provides flexibility to choose exactly what you need rather than being locked into fixed packages. Most importantly, eSIM offers significant savings of up to eighty percent compared to traditional roaming, making it the economical choice for most travelers.

Real Price Comparison

Let's look at a concrete example of a ten-day trip to Italy requiring five gigabytes of data. With regular roaming from an average Israeli carrier, you would pay either ten days times fifty shekels equaling five hundred shekels, or alternatively a weekly package of two hundred shekels plus three extra days at one hundred fifty shekels totaling three hundred fifty shekels. In contrast, a Sparks eSIM for Italy provides five gigabytes valid for sixty days at only two thousand seventy shekels, resulting in savings of up to seventy percent.

Additional Benefits Beyond Price

Using eSIM allows you to keep your Israeli number active for SMS and calls while data goes through the eSIM, giving you the best of both worlds. You can forget about the stress of removing the SIM tray and swapping cards at the airport when you're already rushing to catch your flight. At Sparks, all packages are valid for a minimum of forty-five days, so if you didn't finish the data you can use it on your next trip. Perhaps most importantly, you get a local connection through the best regional carrier, providing the same quality service as local residents enjoy.

When Roaming Might Be Better

There are rare cases where roaming is preferable to eSIM. Very short trips of one to two days might not justify the effort of setting up an eSIM. If your carrier offers a special promotional deal that makes roaming unusually cheap, it might be worth considering. Additionally, if your device doesn't support eSIM technology, you'll need to stick with traditional options like roaming or buying a local SIM card.

Making the Final Decision

For the vast majority of travelers, eSIM is the smart and more economical solution. With Sparks eSIM you get savings of up to eighty percent compared to roaming. You enjoy maximum flexibility in choosing your data amount and usage period. The quality coverage with local carriers ensures reliable connectivity throughout your trip. The long validity period combined with reusable features means you get true value for money. The bottom line is clear: if you're flying for more than three days, eSIM pays much more than traditional roaming options.
